item.title: Bank of Canada Committed to Keeping CPI Close to 2%, Gov. Macklem Says
item.description: Senior officials have agreed to avoid overreacting to the war in Iran’s immediate effects on inflation, “but if energy prices stay high, we will not let their effects become persistent inflation,” Macklem said Monday in testimony to lawmakers.

item.title: Health Officials Work to Contain Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
item.description: The virus spread by rodents killed three people and sickened three others, the World Health Organization said.

item.title: EU Trade Chief Plans to Meet U.S. Counterpart in Paris
item.description: European officials are reeling from another American threat to raise tariffs on exports to the U.S. despite a trade agreement reached between the bloc and the Trump administration last year.

item.description: Several vessels were struck amid Iranian threats, while the U.S. sent in destroyers and fought off further attacks.

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Luxury Residential Block in Moscow
item.description: The attack highlighted the capital’s vulnerability to Ukrainian strikes just days ahead of a military parade.

item.title: Americans Will Do Anything to Get Indian Mangoes
item.description: They stalk WhatsApp groups, track flights like anxious parents and leave work meetings to get their fix of the sweet, seasonal delicacy.

item.title: Asia Manufacturing Sector Feeling Strain From Middle East Conflict
item.description: Gauges of factory activity showed more evidence of supply-chain disruptions from the war, with stockpiling buoying production as cost pressures intensify.

item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co., said the spending will be on projects across its upstream and downstream operations through to 2028.

item.description: The president called the plan, which doesn’t involve escorts by U.S. warships, a humanitarian gesture, and said that discussions with Iran continue.

item.description: The Kremlin provides intel to help Tehran target Americans.

item.description: Charges made in New York mean President Sheinbaum will have to choose a side.

item.title: The Archaeologist Caught Up in a Prisoner Swap Between Russia and the West
item.description: Alexander Butyagin spent years excavating in Crimea. His case raised questions over the fate of historical treasures found on occupied land.

item.description: The two servicemembers had been participating in a training exercise and were reported missing Saturday near a training area.

item.description: The agreement is seen as symbolic because of the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.description: The island’s leader scored a point in the battle for recognition, surfacing in Eswatini despite Beijing’s objections.

item.title: Germany Has the Cash to Revitalize Its Economy, but Can’t Seem to Spend It
item.description: While much of the rest of the developed world is swimming in too much debt, Germany is having trouble using just a tiny bit more.

item.description: A part-by-part breakdown of a typical first-person-view drone shows the strength of China’s stranglehold.

item.description: Meet the Kpler data sleuths tracking Iran’s oil tankers and turning maritime subterfuge into a booming business.

item.title: Russia Oil Exports Keep Flowing Despite Ukrainian Attacks
item.description: Kyiv’s audacious campaign of strikes has had only a modest impact on the gusher of oil money that has shored up Moscow’s finances amid the Iran war.

item.description: President Trump’s move to pull troops from Germany adds to a split leaving Europe’s economy and security dangerously exposed.

item.title: Mexican Governor Indicted by U.S. Steps Down
